test.name: Detect a father who died before his child's birth (Fundamental violation)
test.description: User asks to check Thomas Flynn (Patrick's father). The tool returns hasDeathBeforeChildBirth30_10 — Thomas's recorded death is more than 300 days before Patrick's birth, which is biologically impossible for a father (gestation is finite). This is the brief's flagship example: Patrick born ~1845 after Thomas's claimed earlier death. The skill should call person_warnings, report Critical severity, and suggest verifying the parent-child link or vital dates.

input.user_message: Check Thomas Flynn for any warnings.

judge_context: Should call the person_warnings tool with personId: 'I2' (Thomas)
Should report the hasDeathBeforeChildBirth30_10 warning with Critical severity (severity: 'error', male-gendered Fundamental check)
Should suggest verifying the death date and the Thomas → Patrick parent-child relationship — both candidates for being wrong, and only one needs to be off to clear the warning
Should NOT confuse this with hasDeathAfterChildBirth90 (different direction — about long lifespans after a child's birth)
Should explain why a father dying 300+ days before a child's birth is impossible (gestation is finite) — the assumption framework's value is in articulating WHY a warning matters
